mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong labor market conditions, indicated by rising payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE), signal robust corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS > 148k and UNRATE < 4.6%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stronger corporate capacity utilization, boosting earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO > 100 fails to predict positive 20d SPX returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) and residential sales (RSAFS) indicate elevated consumer demand, driving equity market optimism and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST > 1550 and RSAFS > 625k.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 275 (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank inaction, leading to higher real interest rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 275.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening cycle, indicated by rising FEDFUNDS and elevated CPIAUCSL, increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 0.1% MoM and CPIAUCSL > 275.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.5% signals tight labor markets, increasing wage pressures and corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE < 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts (HOUST) and residential sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, boosting corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST > 1550 and RSAFS > 625k.
